title: draba mulliganii
intro: Draba mulliganii, also known as Mully's whitlowgrass, is a rare and endangered annual plant species native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Draba mulliganii can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds, or internally to boost the immune system and alleviate digestive issues.
